content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 12, 1994

Judith H. Creek
2751 Sunrise Drive
Meadow Vista, CA 95722

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-93-351

Dear Ms. Creek:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you concerning your responsibilities under the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1
Your request does not address a pending decision but rather seeks
general guidance. Accordingly, we treat your letter as a request
for informal assistance under the provisions of Regulation 18329.2

This letter confirms that your letter dated September 13,
1993, accurately summarizes the telephone advice I provided to you
on September 7, 1993. You stated that you are an appointed member
of the Placer County Planning Commission and that you are
interested in actively supporting the land use initiatives to be
placed on the ballot next June 1994. You asked if the conflict-
of-interest provisions of the Act would prohibit you from
supporting those land use initiatives. In response to my inquiry,
you informed me that the political committee primarily formed to
support the qualification and passage of the land use initiatives
is not a source of income to either you or your husband, who
serves as the committee treasurer.

As I stated in our telephone conversation, the Act prohibits
any public official from making, participating in making, or
otherwise using his or her official position to influence a
governmental decision in which the official has a financial
interest. (Section 87100.) The Act specifies that an official
has a financial interest if it is reasonably foreseeable that

1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000-18954. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. I-93-351
Page 2


decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable
from the effect on the public generally, on the official or a
member of his or her immediate family or on:

     (a) Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

     (b) Any real property in which the public
official has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

     (c) Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official status, aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars ($250) or more in value provided to,
received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.

     (d) Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.

     (e) Any donor of, or any intermediary or
agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating
two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value
provided to, received by, or promised to the public
official within 12 months prior to the time when
the decision is made.

                         Section 87103.

     From the facts you have provided, neither you nor your
husband has received, or will be receiving, any income from the
campaign committee which is primarily formed to support the land
use initiatives. Thus, under the Act, the initiative campaign
committee is not a "source of income" to you.

     Consequently, absent some other disqualifying economic
interest as set forth in Section 87103, or some direct financial
effect on you personally resulting from county planning commission
decisions involving the land use issues addressed in those
initiatives, you may participate in such decisions.³


_________________________
³    Also, since you are not a candidate for elected office or an
elected officer, the campaign committee is not a contributor,
i.e., a source of contributions, to you. Accordingly,
disqualification cannot be required under Section 84308.

Our File No. I-93-351
Page 3


If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.4

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

[handwritten signature: Deanne Stone]
By: Deanne Stone
Senior Commission Counsel




4 Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries. Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.

sections.question: You asked if the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act would prohibit you from supporting those land use initiatives.
sections.conclusion: From the facts you have provided, neither you nor your husband has received, or will be receiving, any income from the campaign committee which is primarily formed to support the land use initiatives. Thus, under the Act, the initiative campaign committee is not a "source of income" to you. Consequently, absent some other disqualifying economic interest as set forth in Section 87103, or some direct financial effect on you personally resulting from county planning commission decisions involving the land use issues addressed in those initiatives, you may participate in such decisions.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a member of a Placer County Planning Commission can actively support land use initiatives on the ballot without violating conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act, given that the supporting political committee is not a source of income.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The requestor may participate in Planning Commission decisions regarding the land use initiatives because the campaign committee supporting the initiatives is not a source of income to her or her husband, and no other disqualifying economic interest exists under Section 87103.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an informal advice letter confirming prior telephone advice. Dated January 12, 1994. Contains explicit QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections derived from the requestor's September 13, 1993 letter summarizing telephone conversation from September 7, 1993. File number 1-93-351.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You asked if the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act would prohibit you from supporting those land use initiatives.

CONCLUSION: From the facts you have provided, neither you nor your husband has received, or will be receiving, any income from the campaign committee which is primarily formed to support the land use initiatives. Thus, under the Act, the initiative campaign committee is not a "source of income" to you. Consequently, absent some other disqualifying economic interest as set forth in Section 87103, or some direct financial effect on you personally resulting from county planning commission decisions involving the land use issues addressed in those initiatives, you may participate in such decisions.

embedding.summary: The FPPC confirmed telephone advice regarding whether a Placer County Planning Commission member can support land use initiatives without violating conflict-of-interest rules. The FPPC concluded the member may participate in such decisions since the supporting campaign committee does not provide income and no other disqualifying economic interest exists.
